outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Trump opens door to 20-year Iran nuclear suspension in policy shift departing Beijing
outlets.summary: CNN covers Trump's significant policy shift on Air Force One: he is now open to Iran suspending its nuclear program for 20 years (previously demanded permanent ban), and is weighing lifting sanctions on Chinese oil buyers. CNN analysis notes the shift represents the US's most flexible nuclear position since the conflict began.

outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Lawmakers question Hegseth over Iran war cost as Pentagon admits $29 billion price tag
outlets.summary: NPR covers bipartisan congressional grilling of Hegseth over the $29B war cost (up $4B from earlier estimate), shrinking munitions stockpile, and contradictions between public claims and classified intelligence showing Iran retained 70% of its pre-war missile arsenal.

outlets.name: The Washington Post
outlets.headline: China gains major edge on U.S. amid Iran war, intelligence finds; Iran retains 70% of missiles
outlets.summary: WaPo reports US intelligence assessment finding China is exploiting the Iran war to 'maximize its strategic advantage' across military, economic, and diplomatic domains. Separately, WaPo confirmed CIA/DIA assessment that Iran retained 70% of pre-war missile arsenal and restored 30 of 33 Hormuz launch sites — directly contradicting Trump and Hegseth public claims.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Trump says open to 20-year Iran nuclear freeze; weighing China oil sanctions relief after Beijing summit
outlets.summary: Fox News frames Trump's Air Force One policy evolution as strategic flexibility: open to 20-year nuclear suspension 'if it's real,' and considering lifting China oil sanctions. Frames the Trump-Xi summit as productive — Xi pledged not to arm Iran, agreed Hormuz must stay open. Coverage emphasizes Trump's 'win it one way or the other' resolve.

outlets.name: Daily Wire
outlets.headline: LIVE UPDATES: Trump Declares Iran 'Under Control' As He Vows To 'Finish The Job'
outlets.summary: Daily Wire coverage emphasizes Trump's confidence that Iran is 'under control,' his vow to 'finish the job,' and ongoing protests inside Iran demanding an end to Supreme Leader rule. Covers IRGC's 5-day war exercise near Tehran as evidence of Iran's continued aggression.

outlets.name: Newsmax
outlets.headline: 3 GOP Senators Support Dems in Failed Iran War Powers Vote; 52 Senators Demand No Enrichment Deal
outlets.summary: Newsmax reports the 50-49 war powers vote with Murkowski, Collins, and Paul breaking ranks. Also covers bipartisan congressional letter (52 senators, 177 House members) demanding Trump reject any deal allowing Iranian enrichment — creating pressure on Trump's new 20-year suspension offer.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: How Xi-Trump summit failed to yield Iran war breakthrough — Araghchi: 'no trust in US'
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera analysis: Trump-Xi summit produced only symbolic agreement — no binding enforcement mechanism on Hormuz or Iran's nuclear program. Araghchi confirmed US-Iran talks in 'deadlock' over enriched uranium and said Iran has 'no trust' in the US. BRICS failed unified condemnation over Iran-UAE tensions. Trump now open to 20-year suspension — but Araghchi says uranium issue 'not under discussion.'

outlets.name: Bloomberg
outlets.headline: Hormuz Oil Flows Creep Higher as More Supertankers Exit Strait; Iran allows Chinese vessels
outlets.summary: Bloomberg reports Iranian government confirming it will allow Chinese vessels through Hormuz (VLCC Yuan Hua Hu already transited). ~4 supertankers exited since May 10, carrying ~2M bbl/day Iraqi crude. Analysts: 'so low it won't make much difference.' Iran simultaneously seized the Honduras-flagged Hui Chuan near UAE — contradicting Trump-Xi Hormuz agreement within hours.

outlets.name: Euronews
outlets.headline: Underwhelming summit outcome in China brings Trump back to reality on Iran war
outlets.summary: Euronews analysis: Trump-Xi summit described as 'underwhelming' — no binding commitments on Iran, no Hormuz timeline, China confirmed continued Iranian oil purchases. Trump's 20-year nuclear suspension offer (departing Beijing) seen as a significant diplomatic concession. Lebanon-Israel ceasefire expires May 17 with no permanent deal in sight.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: Araghchi: Iran has 'no trust' in US, uranium deadlock; Trump open to 20-year suspension
outlets.summary: Times of Israel Day 77 liveblog tracks the nuclear negotiation 'deadlock' confirmed by Araghchi, Trump's policy shift to 20-year suspension, Lebanon ceasefire fracturing (IDF soldier killed), Netanyahu Jerusalem Day, IRGC war exercise, and intelligence reports of Iran's retained missile capacity. Araghchi also met Indian FM Jaishankar at BRICS sidelines.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: IRGC launches 5-day war exercise near Tehran simulating US invasion; CIA says Iran can outlast blockade
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post covers the IRGC 'Martyred Leader' 5-day exercise near Tehran simulating a US ground invasion. Also covers the CIA/DIA assessment that Iran has retained 70% of its pre-war missile arsenal and can withstand the blockade for months — directly contradicting Trump and Hegseth's public statements to Congress.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Netanyahu UAE claim denied; Khamenei absent 60+ days as AI images substitute for appearances
outlets.summary: Haaretz tracks: UAE denial of Netanyahu's claimed secret visit ('entirely unfounded'); Mojtaba Khamenei's 60+ day absence with AI-generated images substituting for appearances; US intel saying Khamenei 'mentally sharp but isolated'; and Round 3 Israel-Lebanon talks in Washington described by US as 'productive and positive.'

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Araghchi at BRICS: 'Imperial power in decline — Iran will never bow to pressure'; meets Jaishankar
outlets.summary: PressTV leads with Araghchi's defiant BRICS address calling US power in 'decline' and framing Iran's stand as a victory for the Global South. Also covers Araghchi's bilateral meeting with India's Jaishankar, the uranium 'deadlock' (described as Iran maintaining its principled position), and IRGC's readiness declarations.

outlets.name: Iran International
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian privately called IRGC UAE attacks 'madness'; publicly praises national unity
outlets.summary: Iran International reports the widening gap between Pezeshkian's public unity message and his reported private fury: he allegedly called IRGC attacks on UAE 'completely irresponsible and madness' and sought urgent meeting with Mojtaba Khamenei to halt them. Separately, Pezeshkian issued national unity video praising the nation's 'minimal' economic fluctuations during the war.
